note: ITR-4 (Sugam) is a simplified form. Schedules are fewer than ITR-2/ITR-3. Key FY 2025-26 additions: mandatory bank balance in Schedule BP, expanded Schedule HP (2 properties), new restricted Schedule CG for 112A LTCG.

schedules.description: PAN, Aadhaar, personal details, bank account for refund, employer details (if salary), regime selection.

keyFields.notes: Mandatory u/s 139AA. Aadhaar-PAN linking must be completed.
Select from CBDT's business/profession code list. Examples: 09028 — Retail trade (N.E.C.); 13010 — Doctor/Physician; 16018 — Legal services
Business income taxpayers wanting old regime must file Form 10-IEA BEFORE filing ITR. Once new regime is chosen with business income, cannot switch without filing Form 10-IEA.

commonMistakes: Not filing Form 10-IEA before ITR when opting for old regime with business income
Wrong business code selection leading to mismatch with GST returns

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule BP — Details of Income from Business or Profession (Presumptive)
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Mandatory for all ITR-4 filers — captures the presumptive income
schedules.description: The core schedule of ITR-4. Captures gross turnover/receipts from business/profession and the presumptive net income computed under Section 44AD, 44ADA, or 44AE. FY 2025-26 ADDS mandatory disclosure of cash balance and bank balance.
fy202526Changes: Mandatory cash balance disclosure as on March 31, 2026
Mandatory bank balance disclosure across all accounts as on March 31, 2026

keyFields.notes: Select the applicable presumptive section. Cannot mix sections — if both business and profession, only one can be presumptive in ITR-4.

44AD: Gross business turnover during FY 2025-26 (max ₹3Cr for digital-heavy or ₹2Cr otherwise). 44ADA: Gross professional receipts (max ₹75L digital or ₹50L otherwise). 44AE: Not based on turnover — income per vehicle.

keyFields.fieldName: Percentage of Gross Receipts received through banking/digital channels
keyFields.notes: If >95% of receipts are digital/banking, the enhanced turnover limit (₹3Cr for 44AD / ₹75L for 44ADA) applies. This field is critical for applicability of higher limit.

Must be at least 8% of turnover for cash/other receipts, or 6% for digital receipts. If declaring lower, must maintain books and get tax audit done — must then switch to ITR-3. Can declare higher than 8%/6% voluntarily.
Must be at least 50% of gross receipts. Can declare higher.
For Section 44AE. Max 10 vehicles. Income computed per vehicle per month.
Heavy vehicles (>12T gross weight): ₹1,000 per ton of GVW per month. Other vehicles: ₹7,500 per vehicle per month.

keyFields.fieldName: Salary and interest paid to partners (not applicable for ITR-4 individual filers)
keyFields.notes: Not applicable for individual/HUF filers. Relevant only if the person is computing income from a firm (which would use ITR-5).

keyFields.fieldName: Cash balance in hand as on 31st March 2026
keyFields.notes: NEW MANDATORY for FY 2025-26. Disclose actual physical cash balance maintained by the taxpayer as on the last day of the financial year (March 31, 2026). This includes petty cash, cash at counter, etc. Do NOT include cash in bank accounts.
keyFields.complianceNote: Non-disclosure or understating this figure is a red flag and may trigger scrutiny under Section 69A (unexplained cash credits).

keyFields.fieldName: Aggregate bank balance in all accounts as on 31st March 2026
keyFields.notes: NEW MANDATORY for FY 2025-26. Sum of closing balances (as of March 31, 2026) across ALL bank accounts — savings accounts, current accounts, cash credit accounts, etc. Include balances from all banks. Exclude FD accounts separately held (those may be investment disclosures elsewhere).
keyFields.complianceNote: Banks share account balance data with Income Tax Department via Annual Information Statement (AIS). Inconsistency between disclosed balance and actual balance will be flagged.
keyFields.howToVerify: Download account statements from all banks for the period ending March 31, 2026. Sum the closing balances.

commonMistakes: Declaring income below 8% (44AD) or 50% (44ADA) without maintaining books or getting audit
Not disclosing cash balance — this is mandatory from FY 2025-26
Disclosing wrong bank balance — should match actual March 31, 2026 bank statements
Mixing 44AD and 44ADA in the same ITR-4 — only one presumptive section is applicable
Using ITR-4 when turnover exceeds the applicable limit (₹3Cr or ₹2Cr depending on digital %)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule HP — Income from House Property
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if taxpayer owns house property (up to 2 properties allowed in ITR-4 from FY 2025-26)
schedules.description: Captures income/loss from house property. KEY FY 2025-26 CHANGE: ITR-4 now allows up to 2 house properties (expanded from 1). Both properties can be reported — self-occupied, let-out, or deemed let-out.
fy202526Changes: Expanded to accommodate up to 2 house properties (was restricted to 1 in FY 2024-25)

keyFields.notes: Full address including pin code. Used for property tax verification.
Self-occupied: Nil. Let-out: Higher of actual rent or expected rent (municipal value/standard rent).

keyFields.fieldName: Property 1 — Interest on Home Loan u/s 24(b)
keyFields.notes: Self-occupied (old regime): Max ₹2,00,000. Self-occupied (new regime): Not deductible. Let-out: Full interest deductible.
Second property fields added to ITR-4 for FY 2025-26. If you own only 1 property, leave this section blank.

commonMistakes: Attempting to file ITR-4 with 3+ house properties — must switch to ITR-3
Not declaring second property income — two-property rule is NEW for FY 2025-26
Claiming home loan interest deduction on self-occupied property under new regime — not allowed

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule CG — Capital Gains (Restricted to Section 112A LTCG only)
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Applicable ONLY if taxpayer has LTCG u/s 112A (listed equity/equity MF/business trust units) NOT exceeding ₹1,25,000. NEW for FY 2025-26.
schedules.description: FY 2025-26 NEW FEATURE: ITR-4 now includes a restricted capital gains schedule exclusively for LTCG u/s 112A within the exemption limit. Only this specific type of capital gain is allowed in ITR-4. All other capital gains require ITR-2 or ITR-3.
fy202526Changes: Schedule CG (restricted to 112A LTCG) added to ITR-4 for the first time in FY 2025-26

keyFields.value: LTCG u/s 112A — Listed equity shares / Equity-oriented MF units / Units of business trust

keyFields.notes: Aggregate sale value of all equity shares/MF units redeemed/sold during FY 2025-26 with holding period > 12 months

keyFields.fieldName: Cost of Acquisition (Fair Market Value as on Jan 31, 2018 or actual cost — whichever is higher)
keyFields.notes: For shares acquired before February 1, 2018: Cost = higher of actual acquisition cost or FMV as on January 31, 2018 (grandfathering clause). For shares acquired after Feb 1, 2018: actual acquisition cost.
Automatically set to ₹1,25,000 or gross LTCG (whichever is lower). If gross LTCG > ₹1,25,000, the excess is taxable — but then ITR-4 cannot be used. Switch to ITR-2/ITR-3.
keyFields.warningCondition: If CG-LTCG-GROSS > 125000, taxpayer MUST switch to ITR-2 or ITR-3

keyFields.fieldName: Taxable LTCG u/s 112A (should be NIL if total ≤ ₹1.25L)
keyFields.notes: If within the exemption limit: zero. Taxed at 12.5% if any amount is taxable.

importantConditions: Only LTCG u/s 112A is allowed. STCG, LTCG on property, LTCG on debt MF — all require ITR-2/ITR-3
No capital losses being carried forward from prior years
Net LTCG must not exceed ₹1,25,000 (else must use ITR-2/ITR-3)
commonMistakes: Reporting STCG u/s 111A in ITR-4 — not allowed
Reporting LTCG on property sale in ITR-4 — not allowed
Not switching to ITR-2 when LTCG u/s 112A exceeds ₹1.25L
Using ITR-4 when capital losses from prior years need to be carried forward

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule OS — Income from Other Sources
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if any other source income (savings interest, dividends, FD interest)
schedules.description: Same as in ITR-1 — captures interest, dividends, and other miscellaneous income.

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Applicable only under old tax regime. Under new regime, only 80CCD(2) is applicable.
schedules.description: Deductions available under Chapter VI-A. Limited availability in ITR-4 context — presumptive income taxpayers often prefer new regime for simplicity.

keyFields.notes: Available under both regimes. For self-employed/proprietors using 44ADA/44AD: this deduction applies only if they contribute to NPS as a self-employed person. 80CCD(2) is typically for salaried — verify applicability.

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule IT — Advance Tax and Self-Assessment Tax
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if advance tax or self-assessment tax was paid
schedules.description: Captures tax payment challans for advance tax (paid during FY) and self-assessment tax (paid before ITR filing). Presumptive income taxpayers can pay the entire advance tax in one installment by March 15, 2026.

keyFields.fieldName: BSR Code / Challan Number / Date / Amount

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule TDS — Tax Deducted at Source
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if TDS deducted on any income
schedules.description: Captures TDS on salary (from employer), TDS on business payments, TDS on interest/dividends, etc. Must match Form 26AS / AIS exactly.
Summary of income from all sources: Schedule BP (presumptive) + Schedule HP (house property) + Schedule CG (if applicable) + Schedule OS.
Final tax computation: Gross Total Income, deductions, taxable income, tax at slab/special rates, surcharge, cess, rebate u/s 87A, TDS credit, advance tax = net payable/refund.

keyFields.notes: New regime: ₹60,000 if taxable income ≤ ₹12L. Not applicable on tax computed on LTCG/STCG at special rates.
